items.title: Landsdale MP Daniel Pastorelli gets plum WA cabinet spot after Paul Papalia resignation
items.summary: New Landsdale MP Daniel Pastorelli, the former chief of staff to both Mark McGowan and Roger Cook, will be parachuted into cabinet to replace Paul Papalia, the ABC understands.

items.title: Gout Gout ruled out of World Under-20 athletics titles after training injury
items.summary: Australian athletics star Gout Gout is ruled out of this year's World Under-20 titles in Oregon after a training injury in Brisbane.

items.title: Telstra outage leaves elderly woman with dementia 'frightened', as senator's comments labelled 'irresponsible'
items.summary: An SA woman says her elderly mother was left "frightened" during the Telstra outage after a fall. Meanwhile, SA police have said they have no record of any deaths during the network failure, despite a senator's claims.

items.title: Egypt demands FIFA investigate referees after controversial World Cup loss to Argentina
items.summary: Egypt remains incensed at French referee Francois Letexier after his performance in the Round of 16 clash against Argentina at the FIFA World Cup.

items.title: Fremantle's quality talls and settled defence key as Dockers chase first-ever AFL flag
items.summary: Fremantle has lost its 14-game winning streak with a loss to GWS, but what are the keys for the Dockers to keep going and set up a tilt at their first flag? Cody Atkinson and Sean Lawson investigate.

items.title: How Hudson Young and Liam Martin bashed their way to State of Origin glory
items.summary: Liam Martin and Hudson Young don't like to point fingers, unless it comes time to decide which of the New South Wales backrowers is madder.

items.title: Country musician Jeanette Wormald experiences copyright dispute and AI website woes
items.summary: Country-folk musician Jeanette Wormald was shocked when she found a fake AI-generated website "tribute" to her, in place of a site she thought had lapsed. But then she found disputes over her copyright on her music.

items.title: I'd always wanted to carry a baby. Cancer meant I couldn't
items.summary: The grief of losing the chance to have baby is something I expect to carry forever. But I'm becoming more comfortable with the unconventional life I'm building with my partner April.

items.title: Neil the seal 'may have moved on', Tasmanian wildlife experts say
items.summary: Authorities in Tasmania say it appears Neil the seal may have left his resting spot at a Hobart beach and returned to sea.

items.title: Envoy to combat antisemitism calls for independent oversight committee to review ABC's compliance with charter
items.summary: The ABC and SBS were called to give evidence at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion, with the inquiry hearing that a number of complaints had been received about the public broadcasters' coverage of the conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: Democrats' Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner suspends midterm campaign after sexual assault accusation
items.summary: The Democratic Senate candidate in the US state of Maine has suspended his midterm election campaign after a string of controversies, including an accusation of sexual assault.

items.title: FIFA president Gianni Infantino referred to Olympics ethics investigators over Donald Trump's World Cup intervention
items.summary: Gianni Infantino's close ties with Donald Trump continue to land him in hot water after the US President's World Cup intervention.

items.title: Search for alternatives to paraquat continues after usage restricted
items.summary: Australia buys most of its paraquat from China, but is investigating ways to become less reliant on the chemical.

items.title: WA gold project Warrawoona fined $118,000 over threatened bat
items.summary: A Western Australian gold miner has been hit with a fine of more than $100,000 for failing to protect a vulnerable bat species.

items.title: The Desert Champions Way: Outback Camel Trail, 2026, starts with camel races in Jundah
items.summary: "A mate rang me up and said, 'Do you want to have a go?'" That's all it took to get Jake Callagan in the saddle for the Jundah Camel races. The novice camel jockey took to it well, with a swag of blue, red and green ribbons to take home.

items.title: Emmys 2026: Widow's Bay sneaks in, Rob Reiner honoured and more Emmy surprises
items.summary: Plus Connor Storrie nabs his first nom but not for Heated Rivalry, Hacks soars on a swan song and more successes, surprises and snubs from today's Emmy nominations.

items.title: Queensland police check on 21 callers after national Telstra outage
items.summary: Queensland police confirmed multiple emergency calls across the state failed to connect to Triple Zero during the nationwide Telstra outage.

items.title: Albury families concerned over axing of local NDIS-funded program
items.summary: An Albury-based NDIS provider says an upcoming change to NDIS funding is one of the reasons it will discontinue a program that builds skills and confidence in those living with a disability.

items.title: Ukraine's Kostyuk extends Wimbledon run and slams IOC decision on Russia
items.summary: Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk is through to the Wimbledon semifinals. The 24-year-old says she finds it "tough" as her hometown of Kyiv continues to come under attack from Russia.

items.title: Telstra outage: 'Software defect solution' in place after hundreds unable to call Triple Zero in outage
items.summary: Telstra has implemented a fresh "solution" to resolve a secondary software defect that left hundreds of customers unable to make Triple Zero calls during a nationwide network outage.

items.title: Researchers harvest water from the air to grow native seedlings and rehydrate the Wheatbelt
items.summary: Can water be made from air in WA's Wheatbelt? Researchers are putting an invention to the test.

items.title: NSW coach Laurie Daley slams 'hidden agendas' after State of Origin triumph
items.summary: Laurie Daley has the last laugh over critics who hounded him ahead of the State of Origin decider at Lang Park.

items.title: Wimbledon 2026: British wildcard Arthur Fery sets up semifinal clash with Alexander Zverev
items.summary: After growing up just five minutes from Wimbledon, Arthur Fery is now through to the semifinals at his home grand slam.

items.title: In clash of AFL titans Fremantle and Sydney, who will emerge as premiership favourites?
items.summary: Fremantle take on Sydney at Perth Stadium tonight, in a match boasting storylines as compelling as early-era Melrose Place. Who will emerge from the clash as premiership favourites?

items.title: V/Line train services slowly resume in Victoria following Telstra outage
items.summary: Premier Jacinta Allan has promised two days of free travel on regional rail networks following Wednesday's standstill.

items.title: NT Aboriginal language speakers call for stronger language revival efforts
items.summary: Aboriginal language custodians release a road map for the survival and revival of Northern Territory Indigenous languages.

items.title: Fears for jobs as SunRice cuts shifts due to drought and water buybacks
items.summary: Communities in southern NSW fear large cutbacks to rice processing could put hundreds of people out of work and decimate regional economies.

items.title: Adelaide automotive dismantler penalised $25k for not giving adequate meal breaks
items.summary: An automotive industry group made threats "to create fear" in a man who had brought legal action against his employer for not giving him adequate lunch breaks, a tribunal judgement has found.

items.title: World Cup: Racist commentary called out in media coverage, hurting diversity at the top
items.summary: Leading experts say racial stereotypes in World Cup coverage can be damaging for athletes' leadership aspirations and have an impact on Black communities more broadly.

items.title: Anthony Albanese says India a 'top-tier security partner' for Australia as nations deepen defence ties
items.summary: Anthony Albanese says Australia will increase coordination with India's military forces following talks with the nation's Prime Minister Narendra Modi in Melbourne.

items.title: Birds in Melbourne's west have 'highly elevated' PFAS in their blood, study finds
items.summary: Blood samples found levels of the "forever chemicals" in sparrows near contaminated industrial and military sites were 10 times higher than those in a comparison population.

items.title: State of Origin: Queensland Maroons' furious flair comes back to bite them
items.summary: The Maroons under Billy Slater have gone all in on speed, attack and flair. On the biggest stage in the Origin decider, when things went against them, it all turned a bit too frantic.

items.title: Aboriginal activist Ruth Langford tells Tasmanian court of obligation to prevent 'slaughtered' forests
items.summary: An Aboriginal activist has told a Tasmanian court she has a cultural obligation to "protect lands that are being slaughtered", after she was charged with trespassing while protesting in a logging coupe.

items.title: Company behind rejected Hobart 'Stadium 2.0' concept in liquidation
items.summary: Tasmanian developer Dean Coleman's company, which was involved in the rejected 'Stadium 2.0' plan for Hobart, is in liquidation. He's now involved in a separate development proposal at Regatta Point.

items.title: Report shows ambulance call-outs plummet during initial years of Richmond's medically supervised injecting room
items.summary: Data from a new paper shows the number of heroin-related ambulance call-outs dropped from 48 a month to 14 a month in the first five years following the Richmond medically supervised injecting room's opening.

items.title: How snow machines have become the key ingredient for ski seasons
items.summary: Data shows snow depth has been declining in Australia since the 1950s, with resorts turning to snowmaking to guarantee a strong ski season.

items.title: Questions asked about telco regulation after Telstra suffers outage
items.summary: Advocates have demanded changes to how telecommunication companies are regulated and held accountable for outages after Telstra became the latest telco to suffer a large-scale network failure.

items.title: Live entertainment industry calls for government lifeline as more musicals close
items.summary: As two more major musicals bite the dust, the local theatre industry hopes a live performance incentive modelled on a successful UK scheme will provide salvation.

items.title: TV quote quiz: Guess the shows, then put them in order
items.summary: Now for the story of 20 famous TV quotes, and the one quiz that had no choice but to keep them all together.

items.title: Increased demand and new varieties set Australian coffee industry up for expansion
items.summary: Australians are among the world's biggest coffee drinkers but less than 1 per cent of the coffee consumed nationwide is grown locally. That could be about to change.

items.title: 2026 Emmy nominations revealed, with The Pitt and Hacks topping list
items.summary: The Pitt takes the lead as the most nominated series for the 78th annual Primetime Emmy Awards, with Hacks in second place.

items.title: NRL admits bunker missed knock-on in crucial NSW State of Origin try
items.summary: The NRL admits the bunker missed a NSW knock-on in the lead-up to a crucial try that helped the Blues win Wednesday night's State of Origin decider at Lang Park.

items.title: Australians face record-high rents across all capital cities: Domain report
items.summary: Rents are soaring in several capital cities as low vacancy rates collide with rising interest rates and population growth.

items.title: New South Wales keep the faith to write a new destiny in blood after epic State of Origin decider
items.summary: Against all odds and with most of their state doubting them, Laurie Daley's New South Wales side has pulled off one of the greatest wins in Blues history.

items.title: State of Origin quick hits: Jai Arrow presents shield as Queensland dudded by bunker
items.summary: Jai Arrow is the only Queenslander to touch the State of Origin trophy as the Blues dominate the decider, but a NSW great questions a pivotal try. Here are your quick hits from the State of Origin decider.

items.title: ABC rejects claims of antisemitic bias ahead of fronting royal commission
items.summary: The ABC and SBS will face questioning over complaints of bias and antisemitism when executives appear at the royal commission on Thursday.

items.title: Australia issued blunt warning over AUKUS and China's nuclear capabilities
items.summary: Victor Gao, the vice-president of a major Chinese think tank, conducts some sabre-rattling in the wake of China's missile test in the Pacific.

items.title: Byssal mussel project at Cockburn Sound aims to boost ecosystem health
items.summary: A restorative mussel farm could improve water quality and biodiversity in Cockburn Sound and the project leaders say there's no need to stop there.

items.title: Cleary 'silences the critics' as NSW make Origin history with decider win
items.summary: NSW has produced a historic performance to win the State of Origin series with a 30-12 victory over Queensland at Lang Park in Brisbane.

items.title: Second case of H5 bird flu confirmed in SA, as authorities test two further suspected cases
items.summary: South Australia confirms its second H5 bird flu case while Western Australia investigates two more suspected detections, as authorities say the virus has not spread beyond isolated wild birds.

items.title: Court case against chair of Indian steelmaker won't affect Whyalla sale, SA government says
items.summary: The chairperson of one of the bidders for the stricken Whyalla steelworks has been summonsed to appear before an Indian court, but the government says its confident the sale process will maintain integrity.

items.title: China-linked investors' refusal to divest from WA heavy rare earths company 'extraordinary', expert says
items.summary: China-linked investors in a Western Australia rare earths company have yet to comply with federal Treasurer Jim Chalmers's latest order to sell their shares.

items.title: Lithium-ion vape battery sets man's pants on fire in Byron Bay
items.summary: A Brisbane man visiting Byron Bay has been left with burns after a lithium-ion battery ignited in his pocket.

items.title: Investigation into former NACC commissioner Paul Brereton discontinued over cost
items.summary: Investigations into allegations against Paul Brereton will end after the anti-corruption inspector found they would be too expensive to pursue.

items.title: World Cup quarterfinals: When and where as France, England and Argentina take next step
items.summary: The World Cup has whittled 48 teams down to just eight, with the quarterfinals upon us and the finish line in sight. Find out all you need to know about these monumental matches.

items.title: Australian woman arrested in US after allegedly voting illegally in federal elections
items.summary: An Australian woman has been arrested in the United States after an ICE investigation allegedly found she had illegally voted in multiple federal elections.

items.title: Bondi Beach terror attack survivor critical of YouTube's decision to allow 'false flag' video
items.summary: Arsen Ostrovsky has criticised a decision by YouTube to keep a video on its platform, in which creators suggested he had lied about his injuries from the Bondi Beach terror attack.

items.title: The devastation of ACL injuries can get lost in how prolific they are, says Essendon's Emily Gough
items.summary: Essendon AFLW forward Emily Gough said devastating ACL injuries can get lost in the sheer amount of them in women's game.

items.title: Time-keeping technology triggers Telstra nationwide outage. Here's what we know
items.summary: Telstra says Wednesday's nationwide outage was caused by malfunctioning "nodes" that manage time synchronisation within some of the network data centres, but has no idea what caused the timer issues.

items.title: Is AI changing your life in unexpected ways? Should we move faster or more cautiously? The ABC wants to hear from you
items.summary: Most of us never chose to live in an artificial intelligence (AI) world. Yet over the past few years, it has become part of everyday life, changing how we think, learn and communicate.

items.title: Sale of Tasmania's biggest farm, Rushy Lagoon, to UK pine plantation investor approved
items.summary: The sale of Tasmania's biggest farm to the UK's largest forestry investor has been given the green light — with the news greeted by outrage from the state's farmers' group.

items.title: Hikers warned to take Adelaide's peri-urban parks seriously after rescue
items.summary: Three teenagers were rescued from Onkaparinga River National Park unscathed on Sunday, showing how quickly hikers can come unstuck on spectacular walking trails despite their proximity to Adelaide.

items.title: BHP staff confirm historic strike action at mining giant's Port Hedland operations
items.summary: Unionised workers at the mining giant's operations in Port Hedland will down tools for eight hours on July 16.

items.title: Business fined $1,200 after sparking fire ant scare in Central Queensland
items.summary: The unnamed business shipped a pallet of second-hand bricks infested with fire ants to mine sites in the Bowen Basin in mid-2025, sparking four rounds of aerial baiting at a cost of $1 million.

items.title: World Cup: Despite gut-wrenching loss, Egyptian diaspora celebrates historic campaign
items.summary: After initial disappointment after a late loss to Argentina, many Egypt football fans are joyously reflecting on the team's historic campaign, while using the stage to show support for Palestinians.

items.title: Four fined after allegedly illegally fishing in green zone during Queensland competition
items.summary: The group was caught allegedly fishing illegally in a no-take zone during a competition. Four people have been fined almost $15,000.
